item.title: 在 monorepo 中将 Typescript React 库发布到 NPM | Publish a Typescript React library to NPM in a monorepo
item.description:
雅格布·马罗内

发表于 1 月 21 日

在我的博客上交叉发布
你可以在这里阅读

在这篇博文中，我们创建了一个小型 React 库并将其发布到 NPM。 我们将完成执行此操作所需的所有步骤。

让我们开始。

概述

我们将发布的库（从现在开始是package ）是用 Typescript 编写的，与tsup捆绑在一起，使用changeset进行版本控制和发布。

为了测试包，我们使用Next.js创建一个示例应用程序，并使用Vite创建一个示例应用程序。

为了更好地管理这三个应用程序（我们的包 + Next.js 演示 + Vite 演示），我们使用turborepo将所有内容存储在一个 monorepo 中。

包裹

它将被命名为use-last-visit-date并将包含一个用于：

检索上次访问的日期，该日期之前保存到 localStorage。

教程

本指南不会介绍 monorepo 的工作原理，如果这个概念对您来说是新的，您可以稍微阅读一下以了解它。

如果你想跳过它，你应该知道在本指南中我选择 monorepo 只是为了能够在同一个代码库中拥有包本身和演示应用程序。

否则我应该创建 3 个单独的回购协议。

我们走吧！

设置

使用的工具： turborepo 、 pnpm

要创建新的 monorepo，请使用create-turbo并按照提示操作。

笔记：

如果你没有安装pnpm ，你可以按照安装指南安装它

如果需要，您也可以使用yarn或npm 。


 # create a monorepo npx create-turbo@latest # answers ? Where would you like to create your turborepo? ./use-last-visit-date ? Which package manager do you want to use? pnpm

很高兴知道

例如，从 monorepo 的根目录运行turbo build将在每个工作区中执行build脚本。

如果工作区没有build脚本，则该工作区将被忽略。

它们的执行顺序是什么？

本指南的默认设置就足够了，但我鼓励您稍后阅读文档。

清理

  1. 将根package.json name重命名为“root-monorepo”以避免与我们要创建的包发生冲突。

  2. 删除apps/docs目录，我们不会使用它

要创建我们的包运行：

 # go to root of the monorepo cd ./use-last-visit-date # create package directory mkdir packages/use-last-visit-date cd pacakges/use-last-visit-date # init the package with pnpm pnpm init # install needed dev dependencies pnpm add -D react react-dom @types/react @types/react-dom

添加 tsconfig

我们正在使用打字稿，所以我们需要一个tsconfig文件。

幸运的是， turborepo已经为我们创建了一个共享的 tsconfig 内部包。 里面有各种配置可以扩展。

这是使用像turborepo这样的工具的好处之一。

添加tsconfig作为开发依赖

# in packages/use-last-visit-date/package.json "devDependencies": { + "tsconfig": "workspace:*", }

在整个 monorepo 中重新安装依赖项

使用此内容在packages/use-last-visit-date中创建一个tsconfig.json

 { "extends": "tsconfig/base.json", "include": ["."], "exclude": ["dist", "node_modules"], "compilerOptions": { "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"] } }

写代码

创建将包含我们的代码的文件

# run this from packages/use-last-visit-date touch ./index.ts

并填满

import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; const storage = { key: 'last-visit-date', save: (value: string) => window.localStorage.setItem(storage.key, value), retrieve: () => window.localStorage.getItem(storage.key) ?? null, } export const useLastVisitDate = () => { const [lastVisitDate, setLastVisitDate] = useState<null | string>(null); // On page load retrieve the last visit date if exists useEffect(() => { const value = storage.retrieve(); setLastVisitDate(value); }, []); // On page close save current date useEffect(() => { const handler = () => { const currentDate = new Date().toISOString(); storage.save(currentDate); } window.addEventListener('beforeunload', handler); return () => { window.removeEventListener('beforeunload', handler); } }, []); return lastVisitDate; }

然后通过打开packages/use-last-visit-date/package.json更新将导出的文件

- "main": "index.js", + "main": "./index.ts",

是时候在真正的 React 应用程序中测试我们的代码了。

因为稍后我们还将创建一个 Vite 应用程序，让我们为这个 Next.js 应用程序添加一个更好的名称。

将apps/web目录重命名为apps/next并更新apps/next/package.json ：


 - "name": "web", + "name": "next",

然后使用pages/index.tsx中的useLastVisitDate挂钩

const formatDate = (d: string) => { const date = new Date(d); return date.toLocaleString(); }; export default function Page() { const lastVisitDate = useLastVisitDate(); return ( <div> <h1> {lastVisitDate === null && "Hello, it's first time you visit us !"} {lastVisitDate && `Last Visit: ${formatDate(lastVisitDate)}`} </h1> </div> ); }

但是这会抛出一个错误，因为useLastVisitDate没有被定义，而且它是正确的，因为我们还没有导入它。

在我们导入它之前，我们必须将use-last-visit-date添加到apps/next的依赖项中。

所以更新apps/next/package.json ：


 "dependencies": { - "ui": "workspace:*" + "use-last-visit-date": "workspace:*" }

并告诉 pnpm 通过运行“刷新”monorepo 依赖项

# is not important from "where" you run this command pnpm install

现在您可以在pages/index.tsx中导入钩子

// Add at the top import { useLastVisitDate } from 'use-last-visit-date';

并且错误应该消失。

笔记：

如果这没有发生，请尝试在您的代码编辑器中重新启动 TS 服务器。

现在是时候测试它是否有效了。

 # run this from apps/next pnpm dev

并打开localhost:3000 。

你应该看到

Hello, it's first time you visit us !

然后刷新页面，文本应更改为

使用 shell 移动到 monorepo 的根目录并

# go to apps dir cd apps # create a vite workspace pnpm create vite vite-react --template react-ts # install dependencies pnpm install

然后使用src/App.tsx中的useLastVisitDate挂钩

const formatDate = (d: string) => { const date = new Date(d); return date.toLocaleString(); }; export default function Page() { const lastVisitDate = useLastVisitDate(); return ( <div> <h1> {lastVisitDate === null && "Hello, it's first time you visit us !"} {lastVisitDate && `Last Visit: ${formatDate(lastVisitDate)}`} </h1> </div> ); }

但是这会抛出一个错误，因为useLastVisitDate没有被定义，而且它是正确的，因为我们还没有导入它。

在我们导入它之前，我们必须将use-last-visit-date添加到apps/vite-react的依赖项中。

所以更新apps/vite-react/package.json ：


 "dependencies": { ...other-deps, + "use-last-visit-date": "workspace:*" }

并告诉 pnpm 通过运行“刷新”monorepo 依赖项

# is not important from "where" you run this command pnpm install

现在您可以在src/App.tsx中导入钩子

// Add at the top import { useLastVisitDate } from 'use-last-visit-date';

并且错误应该消失。

笔记：

如果这没有发生，请尝试在您的代码编辑器中重新启动 TS 服务器。

现在是时候测试它是否有效了。

 # run this from apps/vite-react pnpm dev

并打开localhost:5173 。

你应该看到

Hello, it's first time you visit us !

然后刷新页面，文本应更改为

概述

现在， use-last-visit-date是一个内部包，它是用 Typescript 编写的，它之所以有效，是因为 Next.js 应用程序和 Vite 应用程序负责将代码编译为 js。

但是如果这些假设不存在，我们的代码也应该运行。

我们需要编译成js。

然后我们可以发布到 NPM。

回顾一下，将包发布到 NPM 包括两个步骤：

  • 将源代码打包并编译为commonjs ，这样谁安装了我们的 packge 就可以导入它
  • 为本地开发设置开发脚本。
  • 发布到 NPM
    • 控制版本控制
    • 控制发布

捆绑和编译

使用的工具： tsup

首先，使用 shell 到packages/use-last-visit-date和

# install tsup as dev dependency pnpm add -D tsup # create a src dir mkdir src # move index.ts to src dir mv index.ts src/index.ts

然后在packages/use-last-visit-date中的packages.json添加一个脚本

{ "scripts": { + "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format cjs --dts" } }

该脚本将使用tsup打包和编译我们的代码，并将文件输出到dist目录。

因此，您应该：

  • 在 monorepo 的根目录下将dist添加到你的.gitignore中，以避免在git push时推送它。
  • 更新package.json main属性，以便安装此包的人将使用捆绑+编译版本的代码。
 # in packages/use-last-visit-date/package.json - "main": "./index.ts", + "main": "./dist/index.js", + "types": "./dist/index.d.js",

因为这个包是一个react包，在开发的时候我们需要安装react和react-dom作为开发依赖。

但是我们不想在打包和编译时将整个react代码注入到我们的包中。

我们想要的是让包的消费者安装它。

因此，将对等依赖项添加到packages/use-last-visit-date中的package.json


 + "peerDependencies": { + "react": ">=18.2.0", + "react-dom": ">=18.2.0" + },

为本地开发设置工作流程

在添加tsup之前， apps目录中的 apps 直接导入源代码文件（扩展名为 .ts）。

因此，当我们更新源代码文件时，我们的apps会收到更新。

现在我们在两者之间添加了一个编译步骤，所以我们需要在每次更新源代码时触发一个新的build 。

为此，更新packages/use-last-visit-date中的package.json

 { "scripts": { "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format cjs --dts" + "dev": "pnpm run build --watch" } }

从现在开始，在开发时，我们将使用dev脚本。

让我们测试一个完整的本地开发工作流程。 使用 shell 转到 monorepo 的根目录并运行

这将触发每个具有dev脚本的工作区的dev脚本，在我们的例子中：

尝试更改use-last-visit-date代码中的某些内容并观察更新。

发布到 NPM

每次更新源代码时，您都希望将此更改作为新版本发布。

您还需要一个地方来浓缩链接到每个新版本的更改的描述。

您可以手动完成，但非常繁琐且容易出错。
最好使用为此创建的工具。

这是changeset进入音高的时候。

配置变更集

从 monorepo 的根

# install changeset pnpm add -w @changesets/cli # setup pnpm changeset init
创建1.0.0版本

在packages/use-last-visit-date/package.json中将version设置为0.0.1

然后使用changeset的versionig工具

# run this from root monorepo pnpm changeset # a prompt start ? Which packages would you like to include? # select "use-last-visit-date" with arrows and space # then press enter ? Which packages should have a major bump? # select "use-last-visit-date" with arrows and space # then press enter ? Are you sure you want to release the first major version of use-last-visit-date? # confirm it Please enter a summary for this change (this will be in the changelogs). # write a description # like "First release" # then press enter ?Is this your desired changeset? # confirm it # bump version number # running pnpm changeset version

现在version应该是1.0.0 。

发布

在发布之前检查你的 monorepo 中的每个包都不能发布

在它的package.json中。

这会阻止它们发布到 NPM。

然后更新./.changeset/config.json

 - "access": "restricted", + "access": "public",

并且......最后......我们可以发布我们的包

完毕！

结论

我写文章主要是为了帮助自己的未来，或者帮助我在工作中使用的工具的发展。

如果本文对您有帮助，请点个赞。

你想让我谈谈一个特定的话题吗？

在评论中告诉我！

作为 JavaScript 开发者，我们通常不必自己处理编译器。 然而，了解 JavaScript 引擎的基础知识并了解它如何处理我们对人类友好的 JS 代码并将其转化为机器可以理解的东西绝对是一件好事！ 编码愉快！

一旦被暂停，Tresorama 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，tresorama 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，tresorama 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 tresorama 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 tresorama：

降低 tresorama 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的 tresorama 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Jacopo Marrone

Posted on Jan 21

Crossposted on my blog
You can read here

In this blog post we create a small React library and publish it to NPM. We'll go through all steps required to do that.

Let's begin.

Overview

The library we will publish (package from now on) is written in Typescript, bundled with tsup, versioned and published with changeset.

To test the package we create one example app with Next.js and one with Vite.

To better manage these three apps (our package + Next.js demo + Vite demo) we store everything in a monorepo, using turborepo.

The Package

It will be named use-last-visit-date and will contain a React hook used to:

Retrieve date of last visit, that was previously saved to localStorage.

Tutorial

1. Create a monorepo

This guide will not cover how a monorepo works, if this concept is new to you can read a little about it to get the idea.

If you want to skip that, you should know that for this guide i opted to monorepo only to be able to have the package itself and demo apps in the same codebase.

Otherwise i should have created 3 separate repo.

Let's go!

Setup

Tool used: turborepo, pnpm

To create a new monorepo, use create-turbo and follow the prompt.

NOTE:

If you don'have pnpm installed you can install it following installation guide

You can also use yarn or npm if you want.


# create a monorepo
npx create-turbo@latest # answers
? Where would you like to create your turborepo? ./use-last-visit-date
? Which package manager do you want to use? pnpm

Turborepo will init the monorepo and print

>>> Created a new turborepo with the following: - apps/web: Next.js with TypeScript - apps/docs: Next.js with TypeScript - packages/ui: Shared React component library - packages/eslint-config-custom: Shared configuration (ESLint) - packages/tsconfig: Shared TypeScript `tsconfig.json` >>> Success! Created a new Turborepo at "use-last-visit-date".

Good to know

turborepo expose an executable turbo that can fire a script in each workspace with a specific name.

For instance, running turbo build from root of monorepo will execute build script in every workspace.

If a workspace hasn't a build script, that workspace will be ignored.

In which order they are executed ?

turbo.json is the file where this configuration takes place.

Default setting will be enough for this guide, but i encourage you to read documentation later on.

Clean up

  1. Rename root package.json name to "root-monorepo" to avoid collision with package we are going to create.

  2. Delete apps/docs directory, we'll not use it

2. Create package workspace

To create our package run :

# go to root of the monorepo
cd ./use-last-visit-date # create package directory
mkdir packages/use-last-visit-date
cd pacakges/use-last-visit-date # init the package with pnpm
pnpm init # install needed dev dependencies
pnpm add -D react react-dom @types/react @types/react-dom

Add tsconfig

We are using typescript so we need a tsconfig file.

Fortunately, turborepo already created a shared tsconfig internal package for us. Inside there are various configuraion ready to be extended.

This is one of the beauties of using a tool like turborepo.

Add tsconfig as a dev dependency

# in packages/use-last-visit-date/package.json "devDependencies": {
+ "tsconfig": "workspace:*",
}

Reinstall dependencies in whole monorepo

Create a tsconfig.json in packages/use-last-visit-date with this content

{ "extends": "tsconfig/base.json", "include": ["."], "exclude": ["dist", "node_modules"], "compilerOptions": { "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"] }
}

Write code

Create the file which will contain our code

# run this from packages/use-last-visit-date
touch ./index.ts

and fill with

import { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; const storage = { key: 'last-visit-date', save: (value: string) => window.localStorage.setItem(storage.key, value), retrieve: () => window.localStorage.getItem(storage.key) ?? null,
} export const useLastVisitDate = () => { const [lastVisitDate, setLastVisitDate] = useState<null | string>(null); // On page load retrieve the last visit date if exists useEffect(() => { const value = storage.retrieve(); setLastVisitDate(value); }, []); // On page close save current date useEffect(() => { const handler = () => { const currentDate = new Date().toISOString(); storage.save(currentDate); } window.addEventListener('beforeunload', handler); return () => { window.removeEventListener('beforeunload', handler); } }, []); return lastVisitDate;
}

Then update which file will be exported by opening packages/use-last-visit-date/package.json and

- "main": "index.js",
+ "main": "./index.ts",

It's time to test our code in a real react app.

3. Create demo app workspace - Next

turborepo has already created a Next.js app in apps/web so there is no need to do it.

Because later on we will create also a Vite app, let's add a better name to this Next.js app.

Rename apps/web dir to apps/next and update apps/next/package.json :


- "name": "web",
+ "name": "next",

Then consume the useLastVisitDate hook in pages/index.tsx

const formatDate = (d: string) => { const date = new Date(d); return date.toLocaleString();
}; export default function Page() { const lastVisitDate = useLastVisitDate(); return ( <div> <h1> {lastVisitDate === null && "Hello, it's first time you visit us !"} {lastVisitDate && `Last Visit: ${formatDate(lastVisitDate)}`} </h1> </div> );
}

But this will throw an error because useLastVisitDate is not defined, and it's correct because we haven't imported it yet.

Before we can import it we must add the use-last-visit-date to dependencies of apps/next.

So update apps/next/package.json:


"dependencies": {
- "ui": "workspace:*"
+ "use-last-visit-date": "workspace:*"
}

and tell pnpm to "refresh" monorepo dependencies by running

# is not important from "where" you run this command
pnpm install

Now you can import the hook in pages/index.tsx

// Add at the top
import { useLastVisitDate } from 'use-last-visit-date';

and errors should disappears.

NOTE:

If this is not happening, try Restarting TS server in your code editor.

Now it's time to test if it's working.

# run this from apps/next
pnpm dev

And open localhost:3000.

You should see

Hello, it's first time you visit us !

Then refresh the page and text should be changed to

4. Create demo app workspace - Vite

Move with shell to root of monorepo and

# go to apps dir
cd apps # create a vite workspace
pnpm create vite vite-react --template react-ts # install dependencies
pnpm install

Then consume the useLastVisitDate hook in src/App.tsx

const formatDate = (d: string) => { const date = new Date(d); return date.toLocaleString();
}; export default function Page() { const lastVisitDate = useLastVisitDate(); return ( <div> <h1> {lastVisitDate === null && "Hello, it's first time you visit us !"} {lastVisitDate && `Last Visit: ${formatDate(lastVisitDate)}`} </h1> </div> );
}

But this will throw an error because useLastVisitDate is not defined, and it's correct because we haven't imported it yet.

Before we can import it we must add the use-last-visit-date to dependencies of apps/vite-react.

So update apps/vite-react/package.json:


"dependencies": { ...other-deps,
+ "use-last-visit-date": "workspace:*"
}

and tell pnpm to "refresh" monorepo dependencies by running

# is not important from "where" you run this command
pnpm install

Now you can import the hook in src/App.tsx

// Add at the top
import { useLastVisitDate } from 'use-last-visit-date';

and errors should disappears.

NOTE:

If this is not happening, try Restarting TS server in your code editor.

Now it's time to test if it's working.

# run this from apps/vite-react
pnpm dev

And open localhost:5173.

You should see

Hello, it's first time you visit us !

Then refresh the page and text should be changed to

5. Time to publish

Overview

Now, use-last-visit-date is an internal packages, it's written in Typescript and it works because Next.js app and Vite app take care of compiling the code to js.

But our code should run also if these assumptions are not in place.

We need to compile to js.

Then we can publish to NPM.

To recap, publish a package to NPM consists of two steps:

  • Bundle and compile source code to commonjs, so who install our packge will be able to import it
  • Set up a dev script for local development.
  • Publish to NPM
    • Control versioning
    • Control publishing

Bundle and Compile

Tools used: tsup

First, go with shell to packages/use-last-visit-date and

# install tsup as dev dependency
pnpm add -D tsup # create a src dir
mkdir src # move index.ts to src dir
mv index.ts src/index.ts

Then add a script to packages.json in packages/use-last-visit-date

{ "scripts": {
+ "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format cjs --dts"
 }
}

This script,will use tsup to bundle and compile our code, and outputs files to the dist directory.

Because of that, you should:

  • add dist to your .gitignore at root of monorepo to avoid push it when git pushing.
  • update package.json main property, so who install this packages will use the bundled+compiled version of code. 
# in packages/use-last-visit-date/package.json - "main": "./index.ts",
+ "main": "./dist/index.js",
+ "types": "./dist/index.d.js",

Because this package is a react package, while developing we need react and react-dom installed as dev dependencies.

But we don't want to inject the whole react code inside our package when it's bundled and compiled.

What we want is to let consumer of package install it.

So, add peer dependencies to package.json in packages/use-last-visit-date


+ "peerDependencies": {
+ "react": ">=18.2.0",
+ "react-dom": ">=18.2.0"
+ },

Set up workflow for local development

Before adding tsup, apps inside apps directory was importing directly the source code file (with .ts extension).

So, when we updated the source code file, our apps received the updates.

Now we added a compile step in between, so we need to trigger a new build every time we update source code.

To do that, update package.json in packages/use-last-visit-date

{ "scripts": { "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format cjs --dts"
+ "dev": "pnpm run build --watch"
 }
}

From now on, while developing, we'll use dev script.

Let's test a complete local development workflow. Go to root of monorepo with shell and run

This will trigger dev script of every workspace that has a dev script, in our case:

Try to change something in use-last-visit-date code and watch updates.

Publish to NPM

Every time you update source code, you want that this changes will be published as a new version.

You want also a place where to condense description of changes linked to every new version.

You can do it manually, but is very tedious and error prone.
Better to use a tool created for this.

This is when changeset enter the pitch.

Configure changeset

From root of the monorepo

# install changeset
pnpm add -w @changesets/cli # setup
pnpm changeset init
Create 1.0.0 version

Set version to 0.0.1 in packages/use-last-visit-date/package.json

Then use the versionig tool of changeset

# run this from root monorepo
pnpm changeset # a prompt start ? Which packages would you like to include?
# select "use-last-visit-date" with arrows and space
# then press enter ? Which packages should have a major bump?
# select "use-last-visit-date" with arrows and space
# then press enter ? Are you sure you want to release the first major version of use-last-visit-date?
# confirm it Please enter a summary for this change (this will be in the changelogs).
# write a description
# like "First release"
# then press enter ?Is this your desired changeset?
# confirm it # bump version number
# running
pnpm changeset version

Now version should be 1.0.0.

Publish

Before publish check that every packages in your monorepo that MUST NOT be published has

in its package.json.

This prevent them to be published to NPM.

Then update ./.changeset/config.json

- "access": "restricted",
+ "access": "public",

And.... Finally... We can publish our package

Done!

Conclusion

I write articles mainly to help future myself or to help the growth of tools I use in my work.

If this article was helpful to you leave a like.

Would you like me to talk about a particular topic ?

Tell me in the comments !

Classic DEV Post from 2020:

🚀⚙️ JavaScript Visualized: the JavaScript Engine

As JavaScript devs, we usually don't have to deal with compilers ourselves. However, it's definitely good to know the basics of the JavaScript engine and see how it handles our human-friendly JS code, and turns it into something machines understand! 🥳

Happy coding!

item.title: 恢复 IP 地址 | Restore IP Address
item.description:
发表于 1 月 21 日

恢复 IP 地址

/** * @param {string} s * @return {string[]} */ var restoreIpAddresses = function(s) { let ans = []; // if length is greater than 12 that means str cannot be divided in 4 part // if length is less than 4 means not able to generate 4 parts if (s.length > 12 || s.length < 4) return []; function backtrack(start, substrings) { // we have 4 parts of string if (substrings.length == 4) { let strAddress = [...substrings].join("."); // strAddress is of length s + 3(dot) & comparing it with s.length + 3 dots // also means that we have reached the end of string & considered all numbers from string s if (strAddress.length == s.length + 3) { ans.push(strAddress); return; } } // i = start becoz we have to move to nect index every time for (let i = start; i < s.length; i++) { let temp = s.substring(start, i + 1); // temp should be less than 255 if (Number(temp) <= 255) { // temp[0] ==0 means that any part of ip cannot have leading zero // & if so we will not consider that as valid part & move to next index if (temp[0] == 0 && temp.length >= 2) continue; backtrack(i + 1, [...substrings, temp]); } } } backtrack(0, []); return ans; };

类型对于程序员来说是一个日常概念，但要简洁地定义它却出奇地困难。 我发现将 Set用作概念模型会很有帮助。

一个极其强大的打字稿功能是基于控制流的自动类型缩小。 这意味着变量在代码位置的任何特定点都有两种类型与之关联：声明类型和缩小类型。

相关代码 - 1 月 6 日

一旦暂停，zeeshanali0704 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，zeeshanali0704 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

取消发布后，zeeshanali0704 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 zeeshanali0704 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 zeeshanali0704 的方法：

降低 zeeshanali0704 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 zeeshanali0704 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

ZeeshanAli-0704

Posted on Jan 21

Restore IP Address

/** * @param {string} s * @return {string[]} */ var restoreIpAddresses = function(s) { let ans = []; // if length is greater than 12 that means str cannot be divided in 4 part // if length is less than 4 means not able to generate 4 parts if (s.length > 12 || s.length < 4) return []; function backtrack(start, substrings) { // we have 4 parts of string if (substrings.length == 4) { let strAddress = [...substrings].join("."); // strAddress is of length s + 3(dot) & comparing it with s.length + 3 dots
// also means that we have reached the end of string & considered all numbers from string s  if (strAddress.length == s.length + 3) { ans.push(strAddress); return; } }
// i = start becoz we have to move to nect index every time  for (let i = start; i < s.length; i++) { let temp = s.substring(start, i + 1);
// temp should be less than 255 if (Number(temp) <= 255) {
// temp[0] ==0 means that any part of ip cannot have leading zero
// & if so we will not consider that as valid part & move to next index if (temp[0] == 0 && temp.length >= 2) continue; backtrack(i + 1, [...substrings, temp]); } } } backtrack(0, []); return ans;
};

1 Think in {Set}

Type is an everyday concept to programmers, but it’s surprisingly difficult to define it succinctly. I find it helpful to use Set as a conceptual model instead.

#2 Understand declared type and narrowed type

One extremely powerful typescript feature is automatic type narrowing based on control flow. This means a variable has two types associated with it at any specific point of code location: a declaration type and a narrowed type.

#3 Use discriminated union instead of optional fields

...

Read the whole post now!

Maina Wycliffe - Dec 28 '22

Code of Relevancy - Jan 6

item.title: 用一张简单的图片掌握 JavaScript 数组方法 | Master JavaScript Array Methods with One Simple Image
item.description:
您是否厌倦了翻阅文档页面只是为了找到适合您的 JavaScript 代码的完美数组方法？ 不要再观望！ 在这篇博文中，我们将消除数组的复杂性，并将主要方法简化为一个易于理解的图像。 无论您是初学者还是经验丰富的开发人员，本指南都会让您更好地了解 JavaScript 中可用的数组方法以及如何使用它们。 所以，系好安全带，准备好告别困惑，迎接像专业人士一样掌握数组。


通过上图，您可以在一个地方看到主要的数组方法及其相应的描述。 在接下来的部分中，我们将分别深入探讨每一部分。 让我们开始吧！🚀

代码示例：

 let arr = [1, 2, 3]; let count = arr.push(4); console.log(arr); // [1, 2, 3, 4] console.log(count); // 4

代码示例：

 let arr = [1, 2, 3]; let popped = arr.pop(); console.log(arr); // [1, 2] console.log(popped); // 3

代码示例：

 let arr = [1, 2, 3]; let first = arr.shift(); console.log(arr); // [2, 3] console.log(first); // 1

代码示例：

 let arr = [1, 2, 3]; arr.unshift(0); console.log(arr); // [0, 1, 2, 3]

代码示例：

 let arr = [1, 2, 3]; let newArr = arr.map((item) => { return item * 2; }); console.log(newArr); // [2, 4, 6]

代码示例：

 let arr = [1, 2, 3]; let newArr = arr.filter((item) => { return item > 1; }); console.log(newArr); // [2, 3]

第一个数组元素成为最后一个，最后一个数组元素成为第一个。

代码示例：

 let arr = [1, 2, 3]; arr.reverse(); console.log(arr); // [3, 2, 1]

与括号表示法不同，它将为越界索引返回 undefined 而不是空字符串。

代码示例：

 let arr = [1, 2, 3]; console.log(arr.at(1)); // 2 console.log(arr.at(5)); // undefined

原始数组不会被修改。

代码示例：

 let numbers = [2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17]; // create another array by slicing numbers from index 3 to 5 let newArray = numbers.slice(3, 6); console.log(newArray); // [ 7, 11, 13 ]

总之，JavaScript 中的数组是一种功能强大且用途广泛的数据结构，允许开发人员轻松地存储、操作和检索数据。 我希望本指南能帮助您释放数组方法的真正潜力。 但是还有很多我们没有在这篇博客中介绍的数组方法，但是别担心！ 我们将在未来的博客中深入探讨更深入、更高级的主题，敬请期待！

和平出去。 这篇帖子在 2020 年的 DEV 上火了：

作为 JavaScript 开发者，我们通常不必自己处理编译器。 然而，了解 JavaScript 引擎的基础知识并了解它如何处理我们对人类友好的 JS 代码并将其转化为机器可以理解的东西绝对是一件好事！ 编码愉快！

暂停后，dipeshjaiswal 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，dipeshjaiswal 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，dipeshjaiswal 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dipeshjaiswal 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 dipeshjaiswal 的方法：

使 dipeshjaiswal 的所有帖子不那么显眼

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 dipeshjaiswal 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Are you tired of scrolling through pages of documentation just to find the perfect array method for your JavaScript code? Look no further! In this blog post, we're going to take the complexity out of arrays and simplify the major methods into one easy-to-digest image. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced developer, this guide will give you a better understanding of the array methods available in JavaScript and how to use them. So, buckle up and get ready to say goodbye to confusion and hello to mastering arrays like a pro.


With the above image, you can see the major array methods and their corresponding descriptions all in one place. And in the following sections, we will take a deep dive into each one individually. Let's get started!🚀

1. push():

The push() method adds one or more elements to the end of an array and returns the new length of the array.

Code Example:

let arr = [1, 2, 3];
let count = arr.push(4);
console.log(arr); // [1, 2, 3, 4]
console.log(count); // 4

2. pop():

The pop() method removes the last element from an array and returns that element.

Code Example:

let arr = [1, 2, 3];
let popped = arr.pop();
console.log(arr); // [1, 2]
console.log(popped); // 3

3. shift():

The shift() method removes the first element from an array and returns that removed element.

Code Example:

let arr = [1, 2, 3];
let first = arr.shift();
console.log(arr); // [2, 3]
console.log(first); // 1

4. unshift():

The unshift() method adds one or more elements to the beginning of an array and returns the new length of the array.

Code Example:

let arr = [1, 2, 3];
arr.unshift(0);
console.log(arr); // [0, 1, 2, 3]

5. map():

The map() method creates a new array with the results of calling a provided function on every element in the calling array.

Code Example:

let arr = [1, 2, 3];
let newArr = arr.map((item) => { return item * 2;
});
console.log(newArr); // [2, 4, 6]

6. filter():

The filter() method creates a new array with all elements that pass the test implemented by the provided function.

Code Example:

let arr = [1, 2, 3];
let newArr = arr.filter((item) => { return item > 1;
});
console.log(newArr); // [2, 3]

7. reverse():

The reverse() method reverses an array in place. The first array element becomes the last, and the last array element becomes the first.

Code Example:

let arr = [1, 2, 3];
arr.reverse();
console.log(arr); // [3, 2, 1]

8. at():

The at() method returns the value at the given index in an array. Unlike the bracket notation, it will return undefined for out-of-bounds indices instead of an empty string.

Code Example:

let arr = [1, 2, 3];
console.log(arr.at(1)); // 2
console.log(arr.at(5)); // undefined

9. slice():

The slice() method returns a shallow copy of a portion of an array into a new array object selected from begin to end (end not included) where begin and end represent the index of items in that array. The original array will not be modified.

Code Example:

let numbers = [2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17]; // create another array by slicing numbers from index 3 to 5
let newArray = numbers.slice(3, 6);
console.log(newArray); // [ 7, 11, 13 ]

In conclusion, arrays in JavaScript are a powerful and versatile data structure that allows developers to store, manipulate, and retrieve data with ease. I hope that this guide has helped you unlock the true potential of array methods. But there are many more array methods that we didn't cover in this blog, but don't worry! We'll be diving into more in-depth and advanced topics in future blogs, so stay tuned!

Peace out. ✌

This post blew up on DEV in 2020:

🚀⚙️ JavaScript Visualized: the JavaScript Engine

As JavaScript devs, we usually don't have to deal with compilers ourselves. However, it's definitely good to know the basics of the JavaScript engine and see how it handles our human-friendly JS code, and turns it into something machines understand! 🥳

Happy coding!

item.title: 在容器化应用程序的 Rails 7 中运行调试器 | Running Debugger in Rails 7 of containerized app
item.description:
这些天我在使用 docker-compose 的容器化应用程序中使用 Rails 7 和 ruby​​ 3.0。 困扰我很多的问题是在本地主机上调试应用程序。 是的，你可以在docker run命令中运行RSpec的时候调试RSpec，但是直接在本地服务器上调试呢？

解决方案：

在 docker-compose 中，在 rails 服务中，写入：

 web: ... stdin_open: true tty: true

这允许您通过容器的STDIN流向容器发送输入，这在某些情况下很有用。

例如，在容器中运行交互式命令行应用程序时，您可以使用stdin_open: true将命令键入容器并查看输出。

这允许您以类似于终端会话的方式与容器交互。

当容器使用伪 tty 运行时，您可以使用键盘向容器发送输入，容器的输出将显示在您的终端中。 这在容器中运行交互式命令行应用程序时非常有用。

现在，在运行docker-compose up ，您在新的终端会话docker container ls中写入以查找 rails 容器的名称，然后写入docker attach container_name并将debugger添加到您的进程中，当该进程在 docker 中运行时， rdbg将此时停止，现在您可以调试应用程序。 使用c继续下一个断点。

 docker container ls docker attach container_name

要退出附加的容器会话，请按Ctrl+P ，然后Ctrl+Q 。

快乐编码！



原文:

I use rails 7 these days with ruby 3.0 in a containerized app using docker-compose. The problem buzzing me a lot was debugging the app in localhost. Yes, you can debug RSpec when you run the RSpec in the docker run command, but what about direct debugging on the local server?

Rails 7 has a built-in debug gem, and my target was to use this gem, not the old ones like debase, pry, etc.

The problem with docker-compose is that we can log or do console or even do RSpec give input option and system stops while doing RSpec, but rails server starts when we call up on compose, and then we can see logs not control it.

Solution:

In docker-compose, in rails service, write:

web: ... stdin_open: true tty: true

stdin_open: true in a docker-compose.yml file means that the container will have an open STDIN stream when it is run. This allows you to send input to the container over its STDIN stream, which can be useful in certain situations.

For example, you might use stdin_open: true when running an interactive command-line application in a container to type commands into the container and see the output.

tty: true in a docker-compose.yml file means that the container will have a "pseudo-tty" when it is run. This allows you to interact with the container in a way that is similar to a terminal session.

When a container is run with a pseudo-tty, you can send input to the container using your keyboard, and the container’s output will be displayed in your terminal. This can be useful when running interactive command-line applications in a container.

Now, after running docker-compose up, you write in the new terminal session docker container ls to find the name of the rails container and then write docker attach container_name and now add debugger to your process and when that process run in docker then rdbg will stop at that point and now you can debug the application. Use c to continue to the next breakpoint.

docker container ls
docker attach container_name

To get out of the attached container session, press Ctrl+P and then Ctrl+Q.

Happy Coding!
item.author: sulmanweb

item.title: 新手的打字稿 | Typescript for noobs
item.description:
如果你正在阅读这篇文章，可能是因为你想尝试 Typescript，或者像我这样，你必须为了工作而学习它。

我写这篇文章是因为它有助于保留知识，对未来的我来说就像笔记一样。

开始吧....

你为什么要学习打字稿..？

  • 它是社区最喜爱的编程语言之一。
  • 它具有非常高的采用率。 我们可以防止许多 JS 错误 <---- 这个是相关的。
  • 更好的开发体验 <---- 我不确定。
  • 更少的错误 <---- 这个是真的。

嗯，在某种程度上，typescript 是 javascript 的 poke-evolution。
打字稿...它有什么帮助？

在编辑器中运行。

  • 您可以找到拼写错误、错误的函数调用。
  • 强迫你写出完美的代码

    • 在几秒钟内，它们允许我们验证我们的代码是否按照我们认为的那样进行。

    验证我们的系统是否正常工作需要几分钟时间。

  • 我们可以找到不同的边界情况。

    • 需要几个小时来验证我们是否遵循了我们团队的做法和标准。

    打字稿......它是如何工作的？

    好吧，浏览器和 Node.js 本身不识别 TypeScript 文件，您需要做的是将代码从 .ts 转换为 .js。

    在我们的 .ts 文件中，我们将拥有类、接口、模块和类型。 转译器会将代码转换为能够在任何地方运行的 .js。 此外，转译器允许我们选择要将代码翻译成哪个版本的 ECMAScript。

    因此，您可以使用 npm 安装 typescript，建议使用标志 --dev-save 安装它

    npm install typescript --dev-save

    安装完成后，您应该运行下一个

    npx tsc --init

    这将创建我们的 tsconfig.json，您可以使用此文件自定义您的 ts 环境

    额外步骤之一 [类型注释]

    对于打字稿，定义数据类型非常重要，以至于自动假定数据类型，这被称为“推断类型”。

    但我们最好设置类型，我们可以按如下方式进行

    数字

     //type inferred let dato = 100 dato = 30 console.log("dato:",dato) //type asigned let dato2:number = 20 dato2 = 30 console.log("dato2 :",dato2) //type asigned but not defined let dato3:number; //typescript want to prevent an undefined on dato3 console.log("dato3:",dato3)

    记住
    number !== Number
    布尔值

     // Type inferred let isReady = true // Type asigned let isEnable: boolean=true // Cannot be null or undefind let isNew:boolean // = undefined

    字符串

    // Type inferred let myString = 'hello'; // Type asigned let myString2:string = 'hello'; // '' <--- string let anotherString:string = "hello :D"; // "" <--- string let lolesString:string = `this is a string to ${myString}`; // ``<---- string

    数组

    // Type inferred // typescript is inferring that we only have numeric values in the array let arrNumbers = [1,2,43,3,5,34,3,23,2,2] // we can only use array methods with numbers arrNumbers.push(2) // typescript is inferring that we have numeric, string and boolean values in the array let arrAnother = [4,3,23,2,2,'sdf',true] // we can only use array methods with numbers, strings and booleans arrAnother.push('lol') let mixed: (number|string|boolean|undefined|Object)[]= ['lol',3,'yes',false, undefined] mixed.push(23) mixed.push('lolxD') mixed.push(true) mixed.push([]) mixed.push({})

    什么是任何？

     //With any we can change the type for ANY one :D let myDynamicVar:any myDynamicVar=100 myDynamicVar='lol' myDynamicVar=[] myDynamicVar={} // any is not recommended to use
    • 此数据类型禁用 TS 类型并允许我们重新分配变量的数据类型。
    • 不建议使用any ，因为我们失去了捕获任何错误的可能性，而且基本上我们使用的是 javascript。 但是，如果我们将代码从 JS 迁移到 TS，它可能会有用，但是一旦我们有了一个使用 TypeScript 的合并项目，我们就不应该使用any .

    我们可以通过使用“as”运算符或使用“泛型”将“any”类型转换为任何其他数据类型。

     myDynamicVar = 'Hola'; const rta = (myDynamicVar as string); console.log(rta); myDynamicVar2 = 1212; const rta2 = (<number>myDynamicVar2); console.log(rta2);

    我们正在寻找一名高级全栈工程师加入开发团队。 前往此处了解更多信息。

    一旦暂停，julio_santacruz 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，julio_santacruz 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    取消发布后，julio_santacruz 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 julio_santacruz 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 julio_santacruz 的方法：

    降低 julio_santacruz 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 julio_santacruz 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    If you are reading this post maybe it's because you want to try Typescript or like in my case you must learn it for a Job.

    I'm writing this post because it helps to retain knowledge and works like notes for the future me.

    Let 's start....

    Why should you learn Typescript..?

    • It is one of the programming languages most loved by the community.
    • It has a very High adoption rate. <---- Look at the next image
    • We can prevent many JS errors <---- This one it's relevant.
    • Better development experience <---- I'm not sure.
    • Less amount of bugs <---- This one it's true.

    TypeScript vs JavaScript

    Well in some way typescript it's the poke-evolution of javascript.
    Typescript it's like javascript with extra steps, security steps, those steps help us to find errors in the development process.. but how do they work?

    TypeScript... How does it help?

    ---> Static analysis (This is where TS plays its role)

    • Run in the editor.
    • You can find typos, wrong calls to functions.
    • Forces you to write well-done code

    ---> Unit Tests

    • In a few seconds they allow us to verify if our code does what we think it does.

    ---> Integration tests

    • It takes a few minutes to validate if our system works.
    • We can find different borderline cases.

    ---> Code review

    • It takes a few hours to validate that we are following the practices and standards of our team.

    TypeScript... How does it work?

    Well, the Browsers and Node.js do not natively recognize TypeScript files, what you need to do is transpile your code from .ts to .js.

    In our .ts files we are going to have the classes, interfaces, modules and types. The transpiler will convert the code into .js that will be able to run anywhere. In addition, the transpiler allows us to choose which version of ECMAScript we are going to translate our code to.

    So, you can install typescript with npm and the recommendation it is install with the flag --dev-save

    npm install typescript --dev-save

    Once it is install you should run the next

    npx tsc --init

    This will create our tsconfig.json, with this file you can customize your ts enviroment

    One of the extra steps [type annotation]

    For typescript defining the data type is very important, to the point of assuming the data type automatically and this is called 'inferred types'.

    but it is better that we set the type, and we can do it as follows

    Number 

      //type inferred 
  let dato = 100
  dato = 30
  console.log("dato:",dato)


  //type asigned
  let dato2:number = 20
  dato2 = 30
  console.log("dato2 :",dato2)


  //type asigned but not defined
  let dato3:number;
  //typescript want to prevent an undefined on dato3 
  console.log("dato3:",dato3)

    remember
    number !== Number
    number it's a type, Number it's a object

    Boolean 

      // Type inferred
let isReady = true

// Type asigned
let isEnable: boolean=true

// Cannot be null or undefind
let isNew:boolean   // = undefined

    Strings 

    
  // Type inferred
  let myString = 'hello';

  // Type asigned
  let myString2:string = 'hello';  // '' <--- string
  let anotherString:string = "hello :D"; // "" <--- string
  let lolesString:string = `this is a string to ${myString}`; // ``<---- string

    Arrays 

    // Type inferred
// typescript is inferring that we only have numeric values in the array
let arrNumbers = [1,2,43,3,5,34,3,23,2,2]
// we can only use array methods with numbers
arrNumbers.push(2)

// typescript is inferring that we have numeric, string and boolean values in the array
let arrAnother = [4,3,23,2,2,'sdf',true]
// we can only use array methods with numbers, strings and booleans
arrAnother.push('lol')

let mixed: (number|string|boolean|undefined|Object)[]= ['lol',3,'yes',false, undefined]
mixed.push(23)
mixed.push('lolxD')
mixed.push(true)
mixed.push([])
mixed.push({})

    What is any? 

      //With any we can change the type for ANY one :D
  let myDynamicVar:any
  myDynamicVar=100
  myDynamicVar='lol'
  myDynamicVar=[]
  myDynamicVar={}

// any is not recommended to use
    • This data type disables TS typing and allows us to reassign the data type of a variable.
    • It is not recommended to use any, because we lose the possibility of catching any error and basically we are using javascript. However, if we are migrating code from JS to TS it can be useful, but once we have a consolidated project with TypeScript, we should never use any.

    We can convert the 'any' type to any other data type by casting it with the 'as' operator or by using '' generics.

    myDynamicVar = 'Hola';
const rta = (myDynamicVar as string);
console.log(rta);

myDynamicVar2 = 1212;
const rta2 = (<number>myDynamicVar2);
console.log(rta2);

    We're looking for a Senior Full Stack Engineer to join the DEV team. Head here to learn more.

    Mubiru Elton Felix - Jan 18

    Once suspended, julio_santacruz will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, julio_santacruz will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by julio_santacruz will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If julio_santacruz is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag julio_santacruz:

    Make all posts by julio_santacruz less visible

    julio_santacruz consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging julio_santacruz will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 2023 年学习 Unity 的最佳资源 | Best Resources for Learning Unity in 2023
item.description:
作为一名编程讲师，学生和毕业生经常问我学习使用 C# 和 Unity 编写游戏的最佳资源是什么。 这篇文章是我目前学习使用 Unity 进行游戏开发的最佳推荐。

学习理念

我在下面提到的所有内容都是我自己尝试并从中受益的，所以请记住，此列表将偏向像我这样的人：

  • 已经了解 C# 编程语言
  • 通过在有指导和支持的情况下完成小项目，但有一些创造性的自由和实验空间来最好地学习
  • 受益于已经提供的艺术、音乐、声音和 3D 模型
  • 从有时间限制的项目中受益，以最大限度地减少范围蔓延

我个人的理念是学习游戏开发应该类似于构建 LEGO 套件。 你应该有一系列预制件和组装它们的说明，但可以自由地试验和尝试不同的配置，看看这些变化如何影响最终结果。

我认为游戏开发是一项实践活动，因此我将课程和项目放在比书本更重要的位置，尽管我阅读了大多数有关 Unity 游戏开发的主要书籍来补充我的知识。

推荐课程

统一学习

我喜欢推动人们使用的第一个资源是Unity 的免费学习途径。

这些途径是一系列视频和项目，旨在教您一系列与游戏开发团队角色相关的技能。

在撰写本文时，目前有 4 种可用途径：

  • 专注于组件脚本和在游戏开发行业工作的初级程序员
  • 其中，我已经完成了 Unity Essentials 和 Junior Programmer 途径并从中受益，尽管我发现 Unity Essentials 的益处要大得多。

    底线：我对 Unity 新开发者的第一条建议是完成免费的Unity Essentials 途径。

    如果您完成了 Unity Essentials 途径，请选择其他途径或继续阅读本文的下一部分。

    如果您愿意为游戏开发课程付费，我强烈推荐 Brian Winn 通过 Coursera 和密歇根州立大学开设的精彩游戏设计和开发与 Unity 2020专业化课程。

    该专业是一系列包含 5 门课程的视频讲座和可下载的项目。 这些项目包含预制资产，您可以使用它们按照课程结构构建自己的游戏。 然后您提交它们以供审核，其他人会玩您的游戏并让您知道他们喜欢这款游戏的哪些方面。

    通常，当我参加 Coursera 课程时，我会尝试尽可能快地完成它，但在这种情况下，我发现自己花了最多的时间来真正探索每个项目并构建一个我满意的小游戏。

    因此，在完成专业化和数字证书后，我有许多工作游戏准备好分享（由于当时的一些 Coursera 错误，我的名字拼写错误）。 更重要的是，如果我愿意，我拥有尝试更大的东西所需的技能和信心。

    这是 Coursera 的另一个专业化或系列课程，首席讲师再次来自密歇根州立大学。 在这种情况下，Jeremy Gibson Bond 将带您了解 Unity 游戏引擎的各种功能，着眼于参加并通过Unity Certified Associate：程序员认证考试。

    我拒绝参加认证，因为它不符合我的个人目标，需要时间学习，费用昂贵，并且需要定期更新。 但是，作为这些课程的一部分，我确实从学习材料中获得了大量价值。

    不幸的是，从 2023 年初开始，Coursera 似乎不再提供这种专业化。但是，如果它重新出现或迁移到另一个平台，我会推荐它。

    完成 C# Unity 游戏开发人员 3D 课程

    我最后推荐的是来自 Udemy 和 GameDev.tv 的名称平淡的Complete C# Unity Game Developer 3D Course 。

    与大多数 Udemy 课程一样，这门课程是非交互式的，这意味着它可以通过观看一系列视频来完成，而无需在项目中积极应用你的知识。 这使得课程可能不如上面列出的 Coursera 选项强大，除非学习者在观看课程时积极练习技能。

    虽然我的建议始终是 Unity 的实际操作，但我发现这门课程执行得很好。 我并不孤单，因为在撰写本文时，该课程有近 200,000 名购买者、4.7 星评级和超过 30 小时的内容。

    该课程目前的售价为 139.99 美元，但对于任何 Unity 课程来说，一个好的一般经验法则是等到它开始销售后再购买（您不需要等待很长时间）。

    最后的想法

    这些是我目前对学习 Unity 的最佳资源的建议。 这些东西帮助我更深入地了解 Unity 游戏开发，并且我从它们中的每一个中都获得了真正的价值。

    您会在其中找到的一个共同主题是：

    • 从对自己的工作充满热情的人那里获得专家指导
    • 积极的动手学习是无可替代的
    • 为了最大化学习，专注于安排内容以及它们如何组合在一起，而不是自己构建内容
    • 使用小的时间盒项目得出一个好的结果，然后继续下一个项目继续学习

    关注我内容的人会注意到我最近写了更多关于 Unity的文章。 这是因为我想成为那些希望进入 Unity 开发的人的资源，因为我想结合我的两个爱好：人工智能和软件开发。

    我希望未来的内容将有助于说明 AI 在有趣和引人入胜的游戏设置中的原则，并且随着我分享更多关于 Unity 的信息，更多的人将从 AI、游戏开发和编程知识中受益。



原文:

As a programming instructor I often get asked by students and graduates what the best resources are for learning to write games with C# and Unity. This article is my current best recommendations for learning game development with Unity.

Learning Philosophy

Everything I mention below is something I have tried myself and benefitted from, so keep in mind this list will be biased towards people like me who:

  • Already know the C# programming language
  • Learn best by accomplishing small projects with guidelines and support but some creative freedom and room for experimentation
  • Benefit from having art, music, sounds, and 3D models already provided
  • Benefit from timeboxed projects to minimize scope creep

My personal philosophy is that learning game development should be similar to building a LEGO kit. You should have a collection of pre-made pieces and instructions on assembling them, but the freedom to experiment and try different configurations and seeing how those changes affect the end result.

I believe game development is a hands-on activity, so I prioritize courses and projects over books, though I have read most major books on Unity game development to supplement my knowledge.

Recommended Courses

Unity Learn

The first resource I like to push people towards is Unity’s free Learning Pathways.

These pathways are a collection of videos and projects built to teach you a series of skills related to a role on a game development team.

At the time of this writing, there are 4 currently available pathways:

  • Unity Essentials focusing on the basics of the Unity editor and how you can use it to build things
  • Junior Programmer focused on component scripting and working in the gamedev industry
  • Creative Core covering the engine’s handling of materials and presentation resources
  • VR Development focused on building VR applications in Unity

Of these I have completed the Unity Essentials and Junior Programmer pathways and benefitted from both of them, though I found Unity Essentials to be far more beneficial.

Bottom line: My number one recommendation for devs new to Unity is to complete the free Unity Essentials pathway.

If you complete the Unity Essentials pathway, take another pathway or move on to the next section of this article.

Game Design and Development with Unity Specialization

If you’re willing to pay for a game development course, I strongly recommend Brian Winn’s wonderful Game Design and Development with Unity 2020 specialization via Coursera and Michigan State University.

This specialization is a series of 5 courses with video lectures and downloadable projects. These projects contain pre-made assets you can use to build your own games following the course’s structure. You then submit them for review and other people play your game and let you know what they liked about it.

Usually when I take a Coursera course I try to fly through it as quickly as possible, but in this case I found myself taking the maximum amount of time to really explore each project and build a tiny game I was happy with.

As a result, I had a number of working games ready to share after completing the specialization and a digital certificate (with my name misspelled due to some Coursera bugs at the time). More importantly, I had the skills and confidence I needed to try something bigger if I wanted to.

Unity Certified Programmer Specialization

This is another Coursera specialization or series of courses, and the lead instructor is again through Michigan State University. In this case, Jeremy Gibson Bond takes you through a tour of the various features of the Unity game engine with an eye towards taking and passing the Unity Certified Associate: Programmer certification exam.

I declined to take the certification since it did not fit my personal goals, would take time to study for, was expensive, and would require regular renewal. However, I did get a large amount of value from going through the study materials as part of these courses.

Unfortunately, it appears that this specialization is no longer offered by Coursera as of early 2023. However, if it resurfaces or migrates to another platform, I would recommend it.

Complete C# Unity Game Developer 3D Course

My final recommendation is the blandly-named Complete C# Unity Game Developer 3D Course via Udemy and GameDev.tv.

Like most Udemy courses, this one is non-interactive meaning it can be completed just by watching a series of videos without actively applying your knowledge in projects. This makes the course potentially less powerful than the Coursera options listed above unless the learner actively practices skills while watching the course.

While my recommendation will always be something very hands-on with Unity, I find this course to be well-executed. I’m not alone as the course has nearly 200,000 purchasers, a 4.7 star rating, and over 30 hours of content at the time of this writing.

The course is listed at \$139.99 at the moment, but a good general rule of thumb for any Unity course is to wait until it goes on sale before buying it (you won’t need to wait long).

Final Thoughts

Those are my current recommendations for the best resources for learning Unity. These things have helped me get deeper into Unity game development and I’ve gotten real value from each one of them.

A common theme you’ll find in these is this:

  • Get expert instruction from people passionate about what they do
  • There is no substitute for active hands-on learning
  • To maximize learning, focus on arranging the content and how it fits together instead of building the content yourself
  • Use small timeboxed projects to come up with a good result and move on to the next project to continue learning

People who follow my content will notice that I’m writing more about Unity recently. This is because I want to be a resource for those looking to get into Unity development and because I want to marry my two passions: artificial intelligence and software development.

My hope is that future content will help illustrate AI principles in fun and engaging game settings and that as I share more about Unity more people will benefit from AI, game development, and programming knowledge.
